title: Pronouns & Verbs
description: Expand your vocabulary with new pronouns (He, She) and verbs (grab, eat). Build longer SVO sentences.
learning_objectives: Use He/She pronoun gestures as subjects
Combine different subjects with different verbs
Build SVO sentences with varied vocabulary

intro_content.title: More Subjects, More Verbs!
intro_content.explanation: Now that you know SVO order, let's practice with more subjects (He, She) and verbs (grab, eat). Remember: the order is always Subject → Verb → Object.

challenges.prompt_spoken: Build the sentence: He grabs ball.
challenges.target_concept_explanation: 'He' is a 3rd person singular subject. With 'He', use the S-form of the verb: 'grabs' not 'grab'.
hints: Start with the subject: Who grabs the ball?
'He' needs the S-form verb. What is the S-form of 'grab'?
Finish with the object: What does he grab?

SUBJECT_HE|BALL|GRABS.feedback: Remember SVO order! The verb 'grabs' comes before the object 'ball'.
SUBJECT_HE|BALL|GRABS.gesture_hint: After HE, sign GRABS, then BALL

challenges.prompt_spoken: Build the sentence: She eats food.
challenges.target_concept_explanation: 'She' is 3rd person singular, just like 'He'. Use S-form verbs with She.
hints: The subject is 'She' — which gesture represents her?
'She' is 3rd person singular. Use the S-form: 'eats'.
What does she eat? Look for a food-related gesture.

SUBJECT_SHE|EAT|FOOD.feedback: 'She' needs the S-form verb 'eats', not the base form 'eat'.

key_takeaways: 'He' and 'She' are 3rd person singular — they need S-form verbs
common_mistakes: Using base form with He/She (He grab → He grabs)
